3 min read
1 hour ago

--

Today I added Amazon Textract to my Rails book app to automatically extract text from book cover images. Simple enough, right? Wrong. Here’s everything that went wrong — and how I fixed it.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Press enter or click to view image in full size

What I Was Building
The flow I wanted:

• User uploads a book cover image

• Shrine saves it to S3

• App calls Amazon Textract on that S3 image

• Textract returns detected text (title, author, etc.)

• Display results for the user to confirm
Step 1: Add the Gem
# Gemfile
gem "aws-sdk-textract", "~> 1.0"Step 2: Create a Service Object
# app/services/textract_service.rb
class TextractService
def initialize(book)
@book = book
end def extract_text
return [] unless @book.bookcover_attacher.stored?
client = Aws::Textract::Client.new(
region: ENV["AWS_REGION"]
)
response = client.detect_document_text(
document: {
s3_object: {
bucket: ENV["S3_BUCKET"],
name: @book.bookcover.id
}
}
)
response.blocks
.select { |block| block.block_type == "LINE" }
.map(&:text)
end
end
A couple of things worth noting:

• return [] unless @book.bookcover_attacher.stored? — this checks that the image is actually saved to S3 before calling Textract. Without this, you'd get an error on books without a cover.

• @book.bookcover.id — in Shrine, .id returns the S3 key (file path).

• We only pass region: and let the AWS SDK find credentials automatically from environment variables.
Step 3: Hook It Up to the Model
# app/models/book.rb
after_save :extract_text_from_coverprivate
def extract_text_from_cover
return unless bookcover_attacher.stored?
lines = TextractService.new(self).extract_text
Rails.logger.info("Textract extracted: #{lines.inspect}")
end
Rails.logger.info just prints the result to your server logs — useful for checking that everything works before building out the actual UI.

Mistakes I Made (So You Don’t Have To)
Press enter or click to view image in full size

❌ Mistake 1: Passing credentials manually
My first version passed access_key_id and secret_access_key explicitly:
client = Aws::Textract::Client.new(
region: ENV["AWS_REGION"],
access_key_id: ENV["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
secret_access_key: ENV["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"]
)This caused a MissingRegionError even though the env vars were set. The fix was to just pass region: and let the SDK handle credentials automatically.
❌ Mistake 2: Wrong AWS region
My .env had AWS_REGION=ap-northeast-2 but my S3 bucket was actually in us-east-2. Textract has to be called in the same region as your S3 bucket. Fixed by updating .env:
AWS_REGION=us-east-2❌ Mistake 3: Textract wasn’t activated
After fixing everything else, I got a credentials/access error. Turns out Textract needs to be explicitly activated in your AWS account — and it’s not free. Head to the AWS console and enable Textract for your account before testing.
IAM Permissions
Make sure your IAM user has AmazonTextractFullAccess attached. You can add it in:
AWS Console → IAM → Users → [your user] → Permissions → Add permissions
Search for AmazonTextractFullAccess and attach it.
